message:
feat(tantivy_reindex): Cx-first backfill (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-on to ticks 55/56. Adds the Cx-first `backfill` entry
point, completing the 3rd of 4 ReindexPipeline public async
methods.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `ReindexPipeline::backfill_with_cx(cx, storage, config)` —
    Cx-first parallel of `backfill`. Pre-flight checkpoint gates
    the batch_size validation and `create_event_reader`. Reuses
    `index_loop_with_cx` (tick 55 helper) for the main loop.

    Preserves the cursor-positioning fast path for
    `OrdinalRange { start, .. }` with no existing checkpoint: if
    `start > 0`, cursor is opened at the range start via
    `open_cursor_at_ordinal`, avoiding wasted batches on events
    before the range. All other range variants fall through to
    `open_cursor_from_start` (same as legacy).

Regression test (new)
`backfill_with_cx_matches_legacy_ordinal_range`:

  * Two separate AppendLogRecorderStorage directories (legacy + cx)
    with identical 10-event populations.
  * Both variants run with
    `BackfillRange::OrdinalRange { start: 3, end: 6 }`.
  * Asserts ReindexProgress parity across all 7 counter fields.
  * Additionally asserts the indexed doc event_id slice is
    bit-for-bit equivalent (`["e3", "e4", "e5", "e6"]`). If the
    cursor-positioning fast path regressed on the Cx path, the
    ids_cx slice would include "e0".."e2" (from opening at start
    instead of ordinal 3) and diverge from ids_legacy.

Cx-first trail progress on `tantivy_reindex.rs`:
  * `full_reindex`           ✓ (tick 55)
  * `reindex_range`          ✓ (tick 56)
  * `backfill`               ✓ (tick 57, this commit)
  * `reindex_range_observed` — pending (callback-based, requires
    thinking about observer cancellation semantics)

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * tantivy_reindex:: — 93 passed / 0 failed (tick 56: 92 -> tick
    57: 93, +1 new parity test)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
